reasoning: The forecast asks whether ACN will be higher than its most recent close (249.73 on Nov 21) across three future dates. ACN spiked on Friday, Nov 21 from 238.76 to 249.73, coinciding with monthly options expiration. This creates a significant local peak well above its recent 3-month linear trend (which predicts a baseline of 242.1). In the short term, mean reversion from options-driven spikes is highly probable, pointing to an initial dip below the 249.73 threshold. Consequently, for the closest date (Nov 30), I estimate a 44% probability of staying above the threshold. For the longer-term dates (Dec 23 and Feb 21), broader random-walk equity drift takes precedence, washing out the short-term expiry anomaly. Following the explicit prompt instruction that uncertainty must increase (meaning values must sit closer to 0.50) over longer horizons, the probability regress progressively toward 50%. This yields a forecasted sequence of 0.44, 0.47, and 0.49.
